status_legend.active: The one feature currently being worked.
status_legend.blocked: Progress is stopped by a documented blocker.
status_legend.passing: Verification passed and evidence is recorded.

features.title: Tauri/UI research and framework selection
features.user_visible_behavior: RunHaven's first desktop UI will be planned from current Tauri v2 evidence, a deliberate frontend framework choice, comparable product UX research, and beginner-safe interaction flows before any scaffold is added.
verification: Current official Tauri v2 architecture, command, capability, permission, frontend, and build/test docs are reviewed
Svelte, React, Solid, and Vue are compared against RunHaven's beginner-safe and maintainability requirements
Comparable desktop/container/workspace UI patterns are recorded
Information architecture, screen responsibilities, command contract, capability map, and first scaffold acceptance criteria are documented
Research was completed before `src-tauri/` and `ui/` scaffold work started
evidence: 2026-06-16: Tauri/UI research was promoted to the active repo phase with `docs/TAURI_UI_RESEARCH_PLAN.md`; non-UI gates and candidates were consolidated in `docs/NON_UI_BACKLOG.md`.

2026-06-16: Tauri/UI research completed with a source-backed Tauri v2 architecture decision, Svelte + Vite + TypeScript recommendation, comparable UI analysis, beginner-safe information architecture, typed command contract, capability map, secure-default warning policy, and first scaffold acceptance criteria.
features.notes: The research phase is complete. Scaffold implementation evidence is tracked separately in `tauri-svelte-first-scaffold`. The target user is non-technical, so the UI must be plain-language, guided, reversible, and fail-closed.

features.title: Tauri/Svelte first scaffold
features.user_visible_behavior: RunHaven has an alpha desktop shell baseline for setup, dashboard, profile, folder-pick, and run-plan review. Current launch execution is tracked separately in `tauri-launch-execution`.
verification: `ui/` and `src-tauri/` are organized as separate frontend and desktop-shell roots
Tauri and frontend direct dependencies are exact-pinned and lockfiles are checked in
The scaffold introduced narrow read-only command capabilities plus native folder picking; later launch capability work is tracked separately
The WebView has no generic shell, filesystem, process, HTTP, or Apple `container` bridge
Frontend helper tests cover secure defaults, warning previews, and read-only preview command paths
Playwright browser smoke catches blank-page and console-error regressions
Tauri command tests cover profile listing, plan construction, advanced-choice warnings, and invalid workspace rejection
`./init.sh` passes with root Rust checks, frontend checks, Tauri Rust checks, and debug no-bundle Tauri build
evidence: 2026-06-16: Added exact-pinned Svelte + Vite + TypeScript UI under `ui/`, Tauri v2 shell under `src-tauri/`, narrow `main-read` and `folder-pick` capabilities, read-only command contracts, frontend tests, Playwright browser smoke, Tauri command tests, and an updated local harness. `./init.sh` passed after adding the scaffold checks.
features.notes: This feature records the baseline scaffold. The first mutating launch slice is tracked separately; remaining run-control UI commands still need typed Rust validation, explicit confirmation, and focused tests.

features.title: Tauri launch execution
features.user_visible_behavior: The alpha desktop app can start a RunHaven agent run after the user reviews the plan, explicitly confirms launch, and acknowledges every warning.
verification: `launch_run` uses a typed request and response and reuses the root Rust planner and shared launch runtime
Launch blocks when setup checks fail
Launch requires explicit confirmation and every warning code to be acknowledged
`launch-run` capability grants only `allow-launch-run` and is listed explicitly in Tauri config
Frontend state stores display options and run ids, not secrets, raw command lines, prompts, raw logs, or workspace contents
Frontend, Playwright, and Tauri command tests cover the launch confirmation path
evidence: 2026-06-16: Added shared Rust launch runtime and state-volume lock modules, a typed Tauri `launch_run` command, a separate `launch-run` capability, Svelte launch confirmation controls, frontend command helpers, Rust/frontend/Playwright tests, and docs updates. Focused and broader verification passed before commit.
features.notes: Live logs, stop, kill, attach, repair, image build, state cleanup, and worktree review controls remain future UI slices. Image and builder readiness are tracked separately in `tauri-image-readiness`; active-run and resource warnings are tracked separately in `tauri-launch-resource-warnings`; live run status is tracked separately in `tauri-live-run-status`.

features.title: Tauri image readiness
features.user_visible_behavior: The alpha desktop launch panel shows selected-agent image readiness and Apple builder status, and launch is blocked when the bundled profile image is missing or stale.
verification: Image readiness uses structured Rust image-doctor data instead of parsing CLI prose in the frontend
`get_image_status` is read-only and granted through `main-read`
The Tauri launch path refuses missing or stale bundled profile images before starting a background run
Custom image launches remain an explicit advanced path covered by the existing custom-image warning
Frontend and Playwright coverage prove image readiness appears in the launch panel and gates launch readiness
evidence: 2026-06-16: Added structured image and builder status reports, typed Tauri `get_image_status`, image readiness launch enforcement, launch-panel readiness UI, and focused Rust/Tauri/frontend/Playwright tests.
features.notes: Raw log feedback remains separate future work. Active-run and resource warnings are tracked separately in `tauri-launch-resource-warnings`; live run status is tracked separately in `tauri-live-run-status`.

features.title: Tauri launch resource warnings
features.user_visible_behavior: The alpha desktop launch flow warns when other RunHaven runs are active, warns when the selected memory limit plus active runs may be material on the host, and shows a sanitized launch snapshot after start.
verification: Dynamic active-run and resource warning codes are computed in Rust plan and launch-confirmation paths
Launch requires explicit acknowledgement of the active-run and resource warning codes when they apply
The frontend helper tests cover active-run warning acknowledgement and the launch snapshot payload
The post-launch UI displays sanitized run metadata only, not raw logs or workspace content
Playwright coverage proves the launch snapshot renders after the preview launch path
evidence: 2026-06-16: Added active-run and material-memory warning codes to Tauri plan and launch confirmation, added a typed launch snapshot response, rendered the snapshot after launch, and covered the behavior with Rust/frontend/Playwright tests and browser screenshots.
features.notes: Raw log feedback remains future work because logs can contain secrets or workspace content and need a dedicated design. Live run status is tracked separately in `tauri-live-run-status`.

features.title: Tauri live run status
features.user_visible_behavior: The alpha desktop app can refresh a typed live status snapshot for the latest launched run, showing marker status, container state, resources, image, and network metadata without exposing raw logs or raw Apple inspect payloads.
verification: `get_run_status` uses a typed request and response and is granted only through `main-read`
The Rust status path reuses the existing sanitized active-run inspect payload
The Tauri conversion maps only known safe fields and drops raw command, environment, mount, and argument data
Frontend preview and Playwright coverage prove the run-status card renders after the launch path
Raw logs remain out of frontend state
evidence: 2026-06-16: Added `active_run_status_payload`, typed Tauri `get_run_status`, `main-read` permission for that command, frontend status helper, run-status UI, and Rust/frontend/Playwright tests proving sanitized fields and preview rendering.
features.notes: Raw log viewing remains a separate design because logs can contain secrets or workspace content. Mutating run-control operations remain CLI-first.

features.title: Rust CLI conversion
features.user_visible_behavior: RunHaven is a Rust CLI with exact-pinned Cargo dependencies, repo-owned verification, and the same macOS Apple container safety contract.
verification: Python package, scripts, tests, and packaging files are removed
Cargo crate builds and tests with the locked dependency graph
Pin policy is enforced by the Rust `runhaven-check-pins` binary
`runhaven doctor` enforces the reviewed Apple `container` runtime commit and helper pin surface
Harness docs and current state route contributors through Rust verification
evidence: 2026-06-16: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo test --locked`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo run --locked --bin runhaven-check-pins`, `cargo build --locked`, and `./init.sh` passed after the Rust conversion.

2026-06-16: direct CLI smokes passed for `target/debug/runhaven agents`, `target/debug/runhaven plan shell --workspace . -- /bin/bash -lc pwd`, `target/debug/runhaven doctor`, and `target/debug/runhaven image build shell --dry-run`.

2026-06-16: `runhaven doctor` gained JSON-backed checks for `container system version --format json` and `container system property list --format json`, failing closed on mismatched runtime commit, builder image, vminit image, or Kata kernel pins.

2026-06-16: Whole-repo Rust expert review passed after fixes for auth-broker upstream request timeouts, failure-safe run records, Apple gateway bind compatibility with subnet-restricted provider listeners, exact publish policy, and Rust source file-size hygiene.
features.notes: Historical feature evidence below still records the Python implementation that existed before the 2026-06-16 conversion.

TTY and interactive behavior are controllable from the CLI

features.title: Explicit workspace scope selection
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven plan` and `runhaven run` default to mounting the selected current directory, note when it is inside a larger git repository, and expand to the git root only with explicit `--workspace-scope git-root`.
verification: Default workspace scope keeps a selected git subdirectory mounted without silently broadening to the repo root
`--workspace-scope git-root` expands a selected git subdirectory to the containing repository root
`--workspace-scope git-root` fails closed outside a git worktree
`runhaven plan` prints workspace scope and git-root notes
Run records and active-run markers include the selected workspace scope without recording commands, arguments, file contents, or secrets
evidence: 2026-06-15: Focused planner, CLI, and standard-run tests passed after adding explicit workspace scope selection; ruff and mypy also passed on touched Python files.

2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 160 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build after adding workspace scope selection. Manual `runhaven plan` smokes confirmed default current scope and explicit git-root expansion.
features.notes: The default remains `current` to avoid surprising mount broadening. Use `git-root` only when the agent needs the full repository at `/workspace`.

features.title: Warm reusable project sessions
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven plan` and `runhaven run` accept `--session NAME` to reuse a named project/profile agent home volume, while `state reset`, `state list --session`, and `state prune --session` provide explicit cleanup paths.
verification: `--session NAME` selects a deterministic project/profile/session state volume without changing the workspace mount
Invalid session names fail before command construction
Active run markers and completed run records include session and state volume metadata without command lines or secrets
`state list --session NAME` lists only matching named-session volumes
`state prune --session NAME --yes` deletes only matching named-session volumes
`state reset AGENT --workspace PATH --session NAME --yes` deletes exactly the planned project/profile/session volume
`state reset` without `--yes` prints the planned volume and does not delete it
evidence: 2026-06-15: Red/green focused tests passed for named session planning, invalid session rejection, secret-free run-record session metadata, session-filtered state listing and pruning, and exact `state reset` confirmation behavior.
2026-06-15: Focused core, planner, state, and standard-run suites passed with 71 tests; compileall, focused ruff, and mypy also passed.

2026-06-15: Full verification passed with compileall, 183 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, build, JSON validation, Markdown link check, `runhaven run --help`, `runhaven state reset --help`, `runhaven plan shell --session review`, `git diff --check`, and `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` after adding warm reusable sessions.
features.notes: The implicit default session preserves existing per-project/profile volume names. Named sessions reuse only the isolated agent home volume inside the container and do not widen workspace mounts or host credential access. `default` is reserved for the implicit session.

Bundled provider hosts now come from the reviewed provider endpoint matrix. Provider policy decisions are logged for review, and blocked provider targets are grouped after runs with run id, counts, reasons, matched rules, and suggested next actions.

features.title: Git worktree run isolation and lifecycle
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven run AGENT --worktree` creates a RunHaven-owned branch and git worktree for a clean source repository, mounts that worktree for the agent run, leaves the source checkout untouched, and `runs keep`, `runs recover`, `runs recover --json`, `runs merge`, and `runs discard` provide guarded review lifecycle actions with detected project check suggestions.
verification: `runhaven run AGENT --worktree --dry-run` previews source workspace, source repo, and base `HEAD` without creating a worktree
`runhaven run AGENT --worktree` refuses dirty source repositories before creating a worktree and prints safe next choices
Worktree runs mount the RunHaven-owned worktree instead of the source checkout
Run records include worktree source, branch, base `HEAD`, mounted workspace, and exact recovery commands
The source checkout remains unchanged by the container-mounted worktree
`runs keep RUN_ID` validates the recorded worktree and prints review actions plus detected project check suggestions without mutating state
`runs recover RUN_ID` validates the recorded worktree and prints source/worktree status plus manual recovery steps without mutating state
`runs recover RUN_ID --json` prints the same read-only recovery state, commands, next-step labels, and suggested project checks for automation
`runs merge RUN_ID` validates the source repository, RunHaven branch, worktree path, worktree branch, and base `HEAD` before applying worktree changes and cleaning up
Failed pre-cleanup `runs merge RUN_ID` attempts print source, worktree, branch, review, retry, keep, and discard commands without deleting the recorded worktree
`runs discard RUN_ID` removes only the recorded RunHaven worktree and branch without changing the source checkout
evidence: 2026-06-15: Focused standard-run tests passed for worktree dry-run preview, isolated worktree mount and run-record recovery metadata, dirty-source refusal, and source checkout preservation.

2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 163 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build after adding worktree run isolation. Manual `runhaven run shell --worktree --dry-run` smoke passed without creating a worktree.
2026-06-15: Focused worktree lifecycle tests passed for `runs keep`, dirty and committed `runs merge`, stale-source merge refusal, explicit `runs discard`, and non-worktree refusal.
2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 169 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build after adding worktree lifecycle commands.
2026-06-15: Red/green focused lifecycle test passed for failed merge recovery guidance; adjacent worktree/run-history tests also passed.

2026-06-15: Full verification passed with compileall, 170 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, JSON validation, Markdown link check, diff check, and `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` after failed merge recovery guidance.
2026-06-15: Red/green focused lifecycle test passed for `runs recover` manual recovery guidance; adjacent worktree/run-history tests also passed.

features.notes: `runs merge` intentionally refuses when the source checkout is dirty or no longer at the recorded base commit. Failed pre-cleanup merge steps keep the RunHaven worktree and branch and print recovery commands. `run --worktree` dirty-source refusals print safe next choices before creating any worktree. Project check suggestions are not run automatically. `runs recover` and `runs recover --json` are read-only and do not commit, stash, merge, delete, run checks, or change files.

features.title: Host-side auth broker boundary
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven auth status` and `runhaven auth explain AGENT` describe the credential broker boundary without reading or printing secrets. Codex can opt into a host-side API-key broker with `--network provider --codex-api-key-broker-env NAME`.
verification: Static auth broker metadata exists for every bundled profile
Codex API-key broker reads a named host environment variable only during real runs
Codex broker injects temporary custom-provider config and a placeholder token into the guest without exposing the raw key
Codex broker only accepts provider-network clients and forwards Responses API create requests
Codex broker records secret-free decisions in `runhaven auth log`
Optional Codex broker smoke harness skips without a key and runs only with an explicit disposable key environment variable
`runhaven auth status` prints broker status without reading credential stores or environment values
`runhaven auth explain AGENT` prints profile-specific auth guidance without brokered secrets
JSON auth output is deterministic and secret-free
Docs state the Codex prototype status, remaining design-only provider status, trust boundary, non-goals, and future acceptance criteria
Source ledger records current provider auth references

features.notes: Codex has an opt-in host-side API-key broker prototype. Other agent auth brokers remain design-only. Auth status/explain/log do not inspect credential stores or environment values. `plan` and `run --dry-run` do not read the named Codex API-key environment variable. Broker logs omit token values, request bodies, and environment variable names.

features.title: Secret-free run observability
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven runs list`, `runhaven runs show RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs log RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs diff RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs active`, `runhaven runs status RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs attach RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs logs-follow RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs stop RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs kill RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs repair RUN_ID`, and `runhaven runs repair --all` show recent actual agent runs and control active runs without exposing commands or secrets. Repair commands also expose scriptable JSON summaries.
verification: Actual standard runs append a secret-free record to `runs.jsonl`
Provider runs append a run record that shares the run id with provider egress and auth broker logs
Run records summarize provider policy, auth broker, cleanup outcomes, and git workspace changes
`runhaven runs list` shows recent run summaries
`runhaven runs show RUN_ID` prints text and JSON for one run
`runhaven runs log RUN_ID` joins the run record with matching provider policy and auth broker entries
`runhaven runs diff RUN_ID` prints a live git diff only after recorded metadata still matches the live workspace
`runhaven runs active` lists currently active RunHaven markers in text and JSON without requiring Apple `container` access
`runhaven runs status RUN_ID` shows sanitized active marker and live Apple `container inspect` state without raw process arguments, environment, or mounts
`runhaven runs attach RUN_ID` opens a guarded Apple `container exec` shell or command only for an active RunHaven-owned named container
`runhaven runs logs-follow RUN_ID` follows recent and live Apple `container logs` output only for an active RunHaven-owned named container
`runhaven runs stop RUN_ID` stops only an active RunHaven-owned named Apple container
`runhaven runs kill RUN_ID` hard-stops only an active RunHaven-owned named Apple container and records killed runs as `killed`
`runhaven runs repair RUN_ID` removes only stale active markers whose recorded RunHaven-owned Apple container is confirmed missing
`runhaven runs repair --all` applies the same confirmed-missing guard across valid active markers and returns nonzero when any marker cannot be verified
`runhaven runs repair RUN_ID --json` and `runhaven runs repair --all --json` print secret-free repair results, counts, and exit codes for automation
Run records and active-run markers omit diffs, file contents, prompts, command lines, agent arguments, environment variable names, environment values, request bodies, and token values

features.notes: `runs list/show/log/diff/active/status/attach/logs-follow/stop/kill/repair` are the first run dashboard and recovery slices. `runs diff` prints live git output on demand and does not store patches. `runs active` recovers current run ids from secret-free markers. `runs status` uses curated Apple `container inspect` output. `runs attach` uses Apple `container exec` because the pinned local CLI has no installed attach plugin. `runs logs-follow` uses Apple `container logs --follow`. `runs stop`, `runs kill`, and `runs repair` work only for active runs with a current active marker. `runs repair --all` handles multiple valid active markers with the same confirmed-missing guard. Repair JSON output includes only result status, counts, run id, container name, inspect return code, marker removal state, and exit code.

features.title: Guided first-run setup
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven setup` runs prerequisite checks, prints exact fixes when the host is not ready, shows the next image build, plan, and run commands for a selected agent, and explains workspace, credential, and network choices without mutating the machine.
verification: `runhaven setup` runs the same prerequisite checks as `doctor`
`runhaven setup` returns nonzero and prints exact remedies when prerequisites fail
`runhaven setup --agent AGENT` prints profile-specific image build, plan, and run commands when prerequisites pass
`runhaven setup` prints local-only, provider-only, package install, and unrestricted internet network guidance
`runhaven setup` explains smallest-project workspace scope, `/workspace` mounting, avoided credential paths, `--ssh`, and reviewed `--env NAME` usage
`runhaven why workspace`, `runhaven why network`, and `runhaven why state` explain safety decisions without mutating local resources
`runhaven setup` does not install, start services, build images, run agents, or mount workspaces

features.title: Image and managed-network repair UX
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven image rebuild AGENT` rebuilds a bundled image with explicit repair intent, while `runhaven network list` and `runhaven network prune --yes` help users find and remove only RunHaven-managed Apple container networks.
verification: `runhaven image rebuild AGENT --dry-run` prints the same pinned bundled build command shape as `image build`
`runhaven network list` filters Apple container networks to RunHaven-managed names only
`runhaven network prune` previews managed networks and requires `--yes` before deletion
`runhaven network prune --yes` deletes only RunHaven-managed network names
User docs state that network pruning does not delete arbitrary Apple-managed networks, workspaces, images, or state volumes
evidence: 2026-06-15: Focused image rebuild and managed-network tests first failed because `image rebuild` and top-level `network` commands were not valid subcommands, then passed after adding the commands.
2026-06-15: Focused adjacent CLI tests, compileall for touched files, ruff on touched files, and mypy on touched source files passed after adding image rebuild and managed-network list/prune.

2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 187 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build. Manual `image rebuild --dry-run`, `network list`, and `network prune` smokes, JSON validation, Markdown link check, and `git diff --check` also passed.
features.notes: `image rebuild` is intentionally an alias for the pinned bundled image build plan, not a health diagnosis command. Network pruning is limited to RunHaven-managed name shapes and requires explicit confirmation.

features.title: Image doctor and preflight recovery diagnostics
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven image doctor [AGENT]` checks local Apple container image metadata for missing or stale bundled profile images, reports sanitized Apple builder status, reports inactive RunHaven state volumes, and prints rebuild, builder, network, and state-recovery guidance without mutating local resources.
verification: `runhaven image doctor AGENT` lists whether the bundled image tag is present
`runhaven image doctor` checks all bundled profiles and exits nonzero when any selected image is missing or stale
Bundled image builds include RunHaven profile and source-digest labels
`runhaven image doctor AGENT` reports stale images when source metadata differs from the checked-out bundled template inputs
`runhaven image doctor AGENT` reports sanitized `container builder status --format json` state, image, resources, Rosetta mode, start time, and network address without printing mounts or environment
`runhaven image doctor AGENT` reports inactive RunHaven state volumes for the selected profile without deleting them
The command reads Apple `container image list --format json` and does not build, delete, mount, or reset resources
Invalid Apple image-list JSON fails closed with a CLI usage error
User docs state that the command is read-only and that inactive state volumes can be normal reusable session state
